changes.before: let that ordinary noise bear the weight of their trap
changes.after: let the ordinary noise cover their trap

changes.before: she had already laid over the lane ... built intention
changes.after: she had laid over the lane ... intention

changes.before: A man tired enough to forget his supper.
Good, she thought. Fear was faster than persuasion.
The room was narrow and forgettable.
Wasteful in one or two places, but elegant.
The balance in the room shifted. She had found the pride under the fear.
The host trigger kept away from the carrier work. Routes stay clean.
changes.after: The part that wakes it kept away from the carrier work. Loads stay clean.

changes.before: Not glory. Inclusion, perhaps. The privilege of serving brilliance.
changes.after: His contempt cracked into something worse: the privilege of serving brilliance.

changes.before: The room was no longer an interrogation room. It was a marked point on someone else's map.
changes.after: The room was a marked point on someone else's map.

changes.before: The escape became nothing but decisions. Left because ... Down because ... Halt because ... Run because ...
changes.after: The escape narrowed to decisions made at a run: left ... down ... halt ... then run ...

changes.before: Not around it. Not within it. Out.
It lasted long enough for Jaina to know the name needed no explanation.
They had the name now. They had the mind on the work. They also had the bitterer thing ... She had wanted clarity to be leverage. Tonight had cured her of that.
changes.after: They had the name now, but the grain had already gone through the gates. Jaina had wanted clarity to be leverage. It had come too late for that.

flags: Kept 'Like a hand over a bell.' — sharp, load-bearing image, not slop.
Kept 'A needle through distance. A chosen point.' — precise kill-strike language earned by Jaina POV.
Kept 'Breach. Seal. Search. Remove.' — clipped operational cadence fits chapter pressure and voice.
Kept 'preserving shell' — allowed by Jaina's full mage register and already setting-native.
Prisoner interruption line 'Far enough that Andorhal is only the throat, not the--' retained; this is genuine cut-off pressure, not ornamental negation.

wordcount: Opening setup was already lean; the useful cuts were abstract phrasing and duplicate framing, not scene structure.
Lines 5-13 carried the first safe trim pocket: ordinary-noise abstraction, stacked ward phrasing, and one surplus character beat.
Interrogation body stayed mostly clean; I cut repetitions and commentary without touching the tactical power shift.
The kill-strike block was not a cut target. It was clean, specific, and already hard-edged.
The chase lost words in the schematic paragraph at lines 219-220; that was the one place pressure turned outline-shaped.
The Andorhal revelation had one removable negation chain and one too-many-summary beat around the close.
Antonidas's stone scene wanted compression around silence and aftermath, not dialogue surgery.
Word count moved 3799 -> 3595. Reduction: 204 words, about 5.4 percent.
hitlist: Checked Dialogue Register Contamination section line by line against all quoted speech.
Checked Document Voice vs Living Voice across narration, especially the cellar analysis and ending summary.
Checked Academic-Discipline contamination; no geometry/mechanics/dynamics/topology phrasing survived this pass.
Checked Verbose Phrases; none of the named hitlist boilerplates survived in v5.
Checked Negation Addiction. Found one clear revelation-chain at lines 269-271 and removed it. Kept the prisoner's interrupted sentence because the cut-off is diegetic.
Checked Expression Decomposition; no 'precursor' or phase-diagram face writing survived.
Checked Narrator Over-Explanation. Biggest survivors were lines 79, 365, and the closing paragraph; all were cut down.
Checked Sentence Opener Monotony. Variety was already strong enough; no opener sweep needed.
Checked Repetition and Monotony. Reduced repeated 'elegant' and trimmed small duplicate emphases in the interrogation and ending.
Checked POV Distance. Removed one explicit thought-tag and kept perception anchored in Jaina's immediate read.
Checked Clinical Anatomy. No medicalized body language survived.
Checked Planning Vocabulary Firewall. No romance-taxonomy or scene-beat language survived.
Checked Stacked Descriptor Clusters. Descriptions were already disciplined; no three-adjective piles needed cutting.
Checked Breath Tell density. One breath-knocked-thin beat was kept because a near-miss and body-collision justify it.
Checked Inanimate-Subject Agency. The chapter mostly grounded atmosphere in labor noise, pressure, and motion rather than haunted-room verbs.
dialogue: Jaina's full mage register mostly passed because it stayed setting-native and embodied rather than modern-scientific.
The main spoken-register survivor was 'host trigger' in Jaina's line; it sounded too report-like and got grounded into lived language.
Changing 'route' to 'road' in the rollout beat kept Havel's plain speech ceiling intact.
The prisoner's 'We build the routes' stayed; smuggling-route language sounds like a man inside a clandestine transport network.
Sylvanas's speech remained clipped, dry, and finished. No bureaucratic bloat survived in her lane.
Corin and Havel already sounded like frightened locals and field hands, not exposition devices.
Antonidas's exchange punished any padding immediately; the silence read better once the narrator stopped decoding it twice.
Sylvanas's water line at the close stayed because it sounds like a person talking in-scene, not an editor landing theme.
metaAudit: Re-read the interrogation block word by word; strongest issue there was psychology restatement, not deeper voice failure.
Re-read the chase's densest paragraph word by word; the repeated because-chain was the cleanest final-slop survivor.
Re-read the Antonidas call and closing page word by word; silence-decoding and thesis restatement were the remaining soft spots.
A genuinely clean chapter still needs suspicion. This one earned its cleanliness only after the second read of the revelation and ending beats.
Most of v4b was already craft-level prose. The correct polish move was to cut residue, not reopen structure.
